puzzle.title: The Servant of Hidden Wisdom
puzzle.intro: In the vast tapestry of Quranic narratives, there stands an enigmatic figure, a servant of Allah graced with divine knowledge and mercy. This mysterious individual crossed paths with one of the greatest prophets, leading to a journey filled with perplexing actions that challenged the limits of human comprehension. Each act, though seemingly unjust, concealed profound lessons about fate, patience, and the unseen wisdom behind divine decree. Who is this shadowy figure, and what truths did they unveil?
clues: I was a servant of Allah, chosen for special knowledge and mercy.
A great prophet sought to learn from me, and I agreed under strict conditions.
During our meeting, I performed three actions that seemed unjust to my companion.
I damaged a boat belonging to poor people, yet it was a hidden mercy.
I took the life of a young boy, though it appeared cruel, it was to spare his parents grief.
I rebuilt a wall in a town that denied us hospitality, revealing a treasure beneath it.

identity.label: Who Am I?
options: Al-Khidr (the righteous servant)
The owner of the two gardens
One of the People of the Cave
The king who argued with Ibrahim
The man who slept for 100 years

trial.label: Trial/Event
options: Meeting Prophet Musa at the junction of the two seas
Building a barrier against Gog and Magog
Being thrown into a great fire
Splitting the sea into two halves
Receiving the tablets of the Torah
trial.answer: Meeting Prophet Musa at the junction of the two seas

location.label: Key Element
options: The junction of the two seas
Mount Sinai
The cave of Thawr
The valley of Tuwa
The well of Zamzam
location.answer: The junction of the two seas

outcome.label: Outcome
options: He explained the divine wisdom behind his puzzling actions and parted ways with Musa
He was granted prophethood after the journey
The boat was destroyed by a storm
The boy was brought back to life
He built a mosque in the inhospitable town
outcome.answer: He explained the divine wisdom behind his puzzling actions and parted ways with Musa

puzzle.arabic: فَوَجَدَا عَبْدًۭا مِّنْ عِبَادِنَآ ءَاتَيْنَـٰهُ رَحْمَةًۭ مِّنْ عِندِنَا وَعَلَّمْنَـٰهُ مِن لَّدُنَّا عِلْمًۭا
puzzle.verse: And they found a servant from among Our servants [i.e., al-Khiḍr] to whom We had given mercy from Us and had taught him from Us a [certain] knowledge. (18:65)
